setting.note: On the mangrove islands of the Guayas river delta near Guayaquil, the mixed-race cholo fishermen and woodcutters live in near-mystical communion with the swamps and their ancient patriarch, Don Goyo, a figure old as the mangroves themselves. Aguilera Malta, a pioneer of Latin American magical realism, roots it in the real estuary: the island villages, the mangrove swamps the cholos harvest, the river and its canoes, and the distant city of Guayaquil whose market and traders press them to cut the forest down.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Demetrio Aguilera Malta, first published 1933. Mapped as a cited work: no text stored, no passages reproduced. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: Don Goyo, Demetrio Aguilera Malta (1933). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author's estate or publisher.

palette.note: The green of the mangroves that are Don Goyo's own body and the cholos' whole world, the swamp the book defends against the axe. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
